_remote_filesystem_note: In v3, the adapter exec config (bundle_path, shell_wrapper, adapter_version) lives in agent-index.json -> remote_filesystem.exec, NOT here. org-config.json only carries org-level metadata: backend identifier, auth method, and connection bootstrap fields. The OAuth client_id/client_secret are inline here (rather than in /org-config/apps/{app}/credentials.json like other apps) because the filesystem itself bootstraps from these values, before aifs_read paths are resolvable. This is the documented exception to the OAuth credential split pattern; see collection-authoring-guide.md.
_capability_providers_note: Added in core 3.10.0. Map of capability-type -> {providers: [...]}. Written ONLY by install-collection Step 5.7 / upgrade-collection Step 6.7 (registration, admin-confirmed, if_revision) and edit-org (manual management incl. deregistration). Consumers read it at runtime per agent-index-core/templates/resolve-capability.md: exactly one provider => auto-bind; zero => fallback behavior from the consumer's requires[] declaration; multiple => V1 surfaces a setup notice (binding interviews are post-V1). provider_config schema is provider-defined (brand-book stores its org-wide brand_usage switch here so consumers never read another collection's setup responses). See capability-provider-spec.md.
_folder_id_note: installed_collections[].folder_id (added core 3.10.1, Option B id-anchored access): the Drive object ID of the collection's code dir /{collection}/. Captured admin-side at install; absent on pre-3.10.1 installs (readers fall back to path resolution). Members are not Drive members and cannot reliably resolve /{collection} by name (bug 20260606-…-db13); addressing by folder_id makes capability sync deterministic. Backfilled by apply-updates Step 1.5 Migration 4 (admin-gated).
_file_end_note: Added core 3.11.0 (file-integrity sentinel standard, standards.md § 'File-integrity sentinel'). org-config.json instances MAY carry a top-level '_file_end': 'AIFS:FILE-END' as their final key — stamped opportunistically whenever a task rewrites the file. Consumers and validators MUST tolerate (ignore) this key. A stamped file missing the key has been tail-truncated.
